Tests bitwise unsigned right shift assignment operation on an object with custom valueOf and toString methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which return `16` and `0`, respectively. The object is then assigned to a new variable `copyObject`, creating a reference to the same object. The code performs a bitwise unsigned right shift assignment operation (`>>>=`) on the `object`, shifting its value by `2` bits to the right. An `assert` statement checks two conditions: first, that the result of the shift assignment on `object` is equal to the result of a simple shift operation on `copyObject`; second, that the value of `object` after the shift assignment is equal to the result of the shift operation on `copyObject`. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily demonstrating the behavior of bitwise operations and object references in JavaScript.
